title: salix arctica var. kophophylla
intro: Salix arctica var. kophophylla, also known as Arctic willow, is a shrub-like plant native to the Arctic tundra regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides pain relief and anti-inflammatory effects. Salix arctica var. kophophylla is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
